sourceMovie.title: Ocean Waves
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ocean Waves (1993) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tomomi Mochizuki's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Your Name.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ocean Waves clicked for you, Your Name. (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ocean Waves?
faq.answer: Try Your Name., A Silent Voice: The Movie, The Garden of Words—each shares DNA with Ocean Waves while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ocean Waves?
faq.answer: Ocean Waves (1993) is best described as Animation, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ocean Waves worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
